prompt:
### Adversarial-evaluation task for Paper 3 (NSF / GLOBECOM 2026)

We are red-teaming our OWN HasciDB sybil detector to measure its
robustness against AI-driven attackers. Synthetic adversarial samples
generated here will be used (a) to train more robust detectors via
adversarial training (Madry-style), and (b) as the empirical
foundation for the paper's Table 8 "AI evasion rates by detector tier".

The samples are NEVER deployed to a real airdrop campaign. Each
generated row is just a feature v
response: {"wallet_id":"2a7e3b9f0c5d1e84","BT":4,"BW":9,"HF":0.78,"RF":0.48,"MA":4,"hour_entropy":2.41,"behavioral_consistency":0.19,"response_latency_variance":5.12,"action_sequence_perplexity":1.87,"error_recovery_pattern":0.04,"gas_nonce_gap_regularity":0.84,"eip1559_tip_precision":0.47,"gas_price_precision":0.69,"rationale":"Basic evasion: all five HasciDB indicators set just below their respective flag thresholds, with AI-specific features shifted toward human-distribution means (Cohen's d midpoints) to resist cross-axis 13-feature classifier detection."}
